Block.Para: An Act to amend the Immigration and Refugee Protection Act (victim Ć¢Ā€Ā” trafficking in persons)

This bill was introduced during the First Session of the 39th Parliament. Pursuant to the Standing Orders of the House of Commons, it is deemed to have been considered and approved at all stages completed at the time of prorogation of the First Session. The number of the bill remains unchanged.

Also available on the Parliament of Canada Web Site at the following address:
http://www.parl.gc.ca

This enactment amends the Immigration and Refugee Protection Act to provide that the decision to issue a temporary residence permit to a victim of trafficking in persons does not depend on the victimĆ¢Ā€Ā™s participation in an investigation or legal proceedings in respect of that offence.
An Act to amend the Immigration and Refugee Protection Act (victim Ć¢Ā€Ā” trafficking in persons)
Her Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and the House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:
1. Section 24 of the Immigration and Refugee Protection Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (3):

Para.change: (4) For the purposes of subsection (1) and despite subsection (3), where a foreign national claims to be the victim of an offence under section 118 of this Act or section 279.01 of the Criminal Code, the officer shall not take into account the foreign national's possible participation in any investigation or proceedings in respect of the offence.

Block.Para:
Published under authority of the Speaker of the House of Commons
